page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The memory configuration provides 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 34 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression tests placing in the top 10% of all tested servers. Memory performance is highly competitive, registering top-tier results in read-only small block cached bandwidth and large block write bandwidth, alongside a strong showing in large block uncached read bandwidth. Single-core performance sits in the middle 50% for stress-ng, highlighting a performance profile optimized for parallelized multi-threaded execution rather than raw single-threaded speed.

The inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage combined with top-tier database operations and Redis SET benchmarks makes this instance highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, I/O-heavy workloads. The balanced resource allocation and high multi-threaded throughput suit it for large-scale web serving, database hosting, and data compression tasks. It is less suited for workloads requiring specialized x86 instruction sets or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose, ARM64-based instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 256.0 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and 34 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-speed database operations, though single-core performance remains average. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local storage deliver strong qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized, I/O-intensive workloads. This server is highly suited for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-throughput ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated cores, fast memory bandwidth, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3
256.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance store storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
34 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and web serving
